item.title: 过去一周的 7 大特色 DEV 帖子 | Top 7 Featured DEV Posts from the Past Week
item.description:
开发团队的格雷西·格雷戈里（她/她）

发表于每个星期二，我们都会根据流量、参与度和一些编辑策划来汇总前一周的热门帖子。 典型的一周从周一开始，到周日结束，但不用担心，我们会考虑本周晚些时候发布的帖子。

如何使用 HTTP 状态码

看看你是否在@pragativerma18中使用了这些状态码。

看看结果如何！

在 React 中防止不必要的重新渲染！

重新渲染是 React 需要更新已经显示的内容时。 这是来自@lico的一个示例，说明为什么在 React TS 中将函数作为 props 传递时不应该重复类型，而是使用它们的类型。

在构建应用程序时，您可能会意识到随着添加更多功能，它的增长速度会变慢。 您可以采取以下措施来减轻@chegerose的影响！

为前端研讨会配置代码空间 - 以及更多！

本教程中有很多有用的提示，所以在深入研究之前不要再浪费一分钟了！

我在 WSL 上的开发工作流程

以下是@nexxeln决定在 WSL 上设置工作流的方式。

这就是我们本周二的每周前 7 名！ 请关注本周的 dev.to 以获取每日内容和讨论……并确保在未来关注这个系列。 你可能只是在里面！

一旦暂停，devteam 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，devteam 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，devteam 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 devteam 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 devteam 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 devteam 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Gracie Gregory (she/her) for The DEV Team

Posted on Aug 23

Every Tuesday we round up the previous week's top posts based on traffic, engagement, and a hint of editorial curation. The typical week starts on Monday and ends on Sunday, but don't worry, we take into account posts that are published later in the week.

How to use HTTP Status Codes

HTTP status codes are short messages that are provided when we interact with a server, showing us what’s happening. See if you’re using those status codes right with @pragativerma18.

Hacking BeReal (On MITM Attacks)

@ozcap wanted to see how much it would be possible to manipulate a social networking app with the help of a software called Mitmproxy. See how it turned out!

Prevent Unnecessary Re-Renders in React!

Re-renders are when React needs to update content that has already been displayed. @adevnadia provides some tips on how to prevent this when possible!

React TS Tips

Here’s an example from @lico as to why you shouldn’t repeat the type when you pass functions as props in React TS and instead use their types.

5 Enhancements to your Node App

As you build your app, you may realize it’s growing slower as you add more features. Here are some things you can do to mitigate that effect from @chegerose!

Configuring a codespace for a frontend workshop — & more!

@blackgirlbytes found the solution to the chaos of workshopping and educating beginner developers to code – GitHub Codespaces. There are so many helpful pointers in this tutorial so don't waste another minute before diving in!

My Dev Workflow on WSL

Windows is not the best choice for programming natively, but that’s why Windows Subsystem for Linux is available. Here’s how @nexxeln decides to set up a workflow on WSL.

That's it for our weekly Top 7 for this Tuesday! Keep an eye on dev.to this week for daily content and discussions...and be sure to keep an eye on this series in the future. You might just be in it!

Once suspended, devteam will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, devteam will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by devteam will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If devteam is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag devteam:

devteam consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging devteam will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: graciegregory

item.title: 为什么我成为一名软件开发人员 - 我的旅程 | Why I Became a Software Developer - My Journey
item.description:
订阅我的时事通讯以了解我所有即将发表的文章。 在未来，我计划做一些赠品并分享一些更酷的独家产品😉

很久以前，在一个遥远的星系中……

从小，我就一直对电脑着迷。 他们似乎有无限的潜力，你可以用他们做很多事情。 很长一段时间以来，我都梦想着从事计算机行业，但我不知道那会是什么，甚至会是什么样子，因为我还年轻，还在努力寻找自己的生活方式。 当我第一次体验在电脑上玩游戏的感觉时，这条路开始变得清晰了很多。

它让我打开了一个全新的世界，这是我做梦也想不到的！ 我什至建造了自己的定制电脑，这对我来说是一次真正的学习体验。 那是我第一次动手组装硬件，然后安装所有驱动程序和软件。 我玩 PC 游戏玩得很开心，仍然有很多童年的美好回忆。

接下来，我被介绍给游戏机，让我的眼界更加开阔。 我还拥有一些 Nintendo Gameboy 和 Sony PSP。 我玩了很多游戏，变得完全上瘾和痴迷。 它确实让我变成了一个极客，让我对编程更加感兴趣。 这些天我在 PlayStation 5 和笔记本电脑上玩游戏。

到目前为止，游戏是我对编程热情的最大贡献者，也是我决定成为一名游戏开发人员的时候。 但是，有一个大问题我不知道该怎么做，甚至不知道我应该学习哪种编程语言......

编程简介

中学

我对编程的第一次介绍是在高中时发生的，但它非常基础，我几乎记不得了。 我们有一个叫做信息通信技术（ICT）的课程，如果我记得我们学习了一些基本的 HTML 和 CSS。 这是我开始学习 Web 开发的时候，我认为这是进入这个行业的一条简单途径。

大学

当我离开高中时，我决定去大学学习信息通信技术（ICT）。 所以基本上我是从我最初在高中那一门课上学到的东西进步的。 在大学里，我学习的最早的编程语言之一是 Visual Basic。 那时我的技术水平很低，我发现这门语言很难学。 在其他课程中，我们还学习了 HTML、CSS 和 JavaScript。

我发现 HTML 和 CSS 很容易学习，因为我有经验。 我们使用的代码编辑器是 Notepad++，它没有现代代码编辑器和 IDE 所拥有的任何很酷的智能感知。 所以没有代码完成、自动更正或任何可用的扩展。 我们必须手动完成所有操作。 我记得在一堂课上，老师希望我们用一些 JavaScript 动画构建一个简单的网页。 我对 JavaScript 完全陌生，仍在学习基础知识。

在浏览互联网时，我想起了一些有一些非常酷的动画横幅的网站。 我们仍在学习 Web 开发的基础知识，但我已经知道如何在 Web 浏览器中使用检查元素来查看和编辑代码。 我能够弄清楚如何复制横幅代码，然后让它在我的简单网页中运行。 当我的老师看到它时，他很惊讶，因为他不知道我是如何做到这样的😅

大学

大学是我的技能真正开始升级的地方。 我正在攻读计算机学位，并且第一次了解如何使用 Java 和 ActionScript。 我们被教导如何使用名为 BlueJ 的 Java 集成开发环境。 我开始学习 Adob​​e Flash 和 ActionScript。 在苹果用乐高积木取代它们之前，Flash 网站仍然很受欢迎。 对于那些不知道 Flash 网站不再可能在 Safari 和 iOS 设备上运行的人，行业转向 HTML5 和 Canvas。

在我的一些大学模块中，我还学习了如何进行 3D 建模和动画制作，这对我成为一名游戏开发人员或走上这条道路产生了更多启发。 我也有一些 Web 开发经验，但我仍然不确定该走哪条路。 尽管我渴望成为一名游戏开发者，但我仍然不确定我是否应该 100% 做到这一点，还是应该成为多面手并走不同的道路。 那段时间我确实做了一些Flash游戏，所以我有一些经验。

我于 2008 年从大学毕业，但不幸的是，这也是大萧条的开始。 失业率很高，我有点迷茫，因为我不知道我应该做什么。

这些是我创建的 Adob​​e Flash 游戏。

我的第一次实习

大学毕业后，我选择在我毕业的同一所大学进行实习计划。 我有幸在伦敦的一家公司实习。 我的网络开发技能仍然很基础，但我可以建立网站。 这是我的第一个角色，我使用 HTML、CSS 和 JavaScript 构建网页以及更新主网站上的内容。 与我一起工作的首席开发人员希望我学习 PHP，并给了我一本书供我学习。

我发现学习这门语言非常困难，因为这本书技术性很强，而且当时没有很多关于 PHP 的在线学习资源。 老实说，我也没有完全被这门语言所吸引，因为我在前端开发方面做得更好，而且对做任何后端工作几乎没有热情。 最后，实习结束后我没有留在公司，因为它正在转变为后端角色，这在当时不是我的事。

我的职业生涯至今

随着实习的结束，我的注意力再次转向找工作。 多年来，我做了自由职业者、合同和永久角色的组合。 我在这方面获得了很多经验，我的编程技能也开始有了很大的提高。 尽管我确实有一些时期在工作或失业之间，但在你的职业生涯中可能会发生很多事情，但这并非一帆风顺。

到 2019 年底，我担任超市送货司机的非技术角色，但这只是兼职工作并没有持续多久……我的计划是重新开始编程，因为我一直退出游戏大约 4 个月，我的编程技能变得如此生疏……但是 2020 年发生了 Covid 大流行，又一次经济衰退……

这实际上是因祸得福，因为我们都被迫在家里呆了几个月。 我利用这段时间来加快我的编程速度，并通过成为一名全栈开发人员来提高技能。 当我提高了 Node.js 的知识后，我终于找到了对后端开发的热情。 当时我没有建立观众群或变得受欢迎的计划，我只是想在社区中活跃起来，这样可以增加我找到工作和被公司关注的机会。

我花了一年的时间再次在科技领域找到稳定的全职工作。 面试感觉就像你在玩大逃杀

这些年来，我接受了一些非常好的和非常糟糕的采访。 老实说，我认为整个面试过程都被打破了，因为它是如此不平衡......无论如何，面试可能会非常竞争，通常只有一个人会得到这份工作，所以你必须尽力让自己成为最好的候选人，所以你是最后一个站在最后的人。 面试告诉我，无论如何你都应该做好准备。 在一些采访中，有人问我是否有什么要展示的，我只能说没有，因为我的作品集很干，没有足够的内容，或者我选择不带笔记本电脑，所以我无法向他们展示我的作品。

这是我创建的 Fortnite Database Battle Royale 应用程序。 你可以在这里在线找到它。

在其他情况下，我完全准备好回答技术问题，但我花了太多时间来解决这些问题，以至于我忘记了为最简单的一般性问题做好准备。 比如“你对公司了解多少？” 以及“你为什么选择应聘这个角色？”。 它们简单明了，但是当您感到压力和焦虑时，您的大脑可能会一片空白，试图回答一个您从未准备好的问题。

我从失败的面试中得到的最大收获是填补任何缺失的技能和知识空白。 始终寻求反馈，这是非常有价值的，您可以使用它为下一次面试做好更好的准备。 专注于你的弱点，让它们成为你的优势。 因此，如果您不擅长数据结构和算法，那么请进行大量练习。 如果您不了解 JavaScript 基础知识，那么请花时间变得更好。 把你的失败变成积极的，把失败变成胜利。

现代找工作的方式

今天作为程序员找到工作可能极具挑战性。

寻找工作的传统方式可能看起来像🥱

  • 另一种找工作的方式可能看起来像💫

    • 公共建筑
    • 创建 SaaS 和 PaaS
    • 撰写技术文章
    • 在 LinkedIn 和 Twitter 上建立网络

    如果您是该行业的新手，并且正在转投科技行业寻找您的第一个角色，我建议您沿着传统路线走另一条路。 这是非正统的，你正在跳出框框思考，做一些能让你在人群中脱颖而出的事情。 我会给出的另一个热门提示是去面试，然后做一个演讲。 想想看，你是品牌，你正试图推销自己和你的产品。

    因此，如果您可以构建几个出色的应用程序，而不是每个人都做的标准待办事项列表应用程序。 你很有可能给面试官留下深刻印象。 如果它是 SaaS 或 PaaS，它正在产生收入并且很多人都在使用，那么你很可能会获得奖励积分。 所有这些都极有可能改善您的投资组合并突出您的技能。

    我今天在哪里

    今天，我仍然在 CGI 作为一名软件开发人员工作，我对前端、后端和移动开发充满热情。 技术写作和内容创作现在也是我生活的重要组成部分，因为我正努力在社交媒体上发展我的品牌和受众。 我有一些正在进行的自由职业和被动收入流，对未来有更多的想法。

    我仍然不是游戏开发人员，但我已经自学了一些 C#，并且我已经了解 Unity 游戏引擎很长一段时间了，其中 C# 是主要的编程语言之一。 因此，即使它没有成为主要职业，它仍然可能是未来非常有趣的副业。 因为试图找到时间做我现在正在做的所有事情已经非常紧张了😅

    谢谢你的时间！

    文字拥有如此大的力量，可以从字面上改变你对现实的看法。 写作是一种令人难以置信的分享思想、知识和学习的媒介，这就是我如此喜欢写作的原因。 连接和网络是发展您的社交网络的绝佳方式，这可以为您打开许多个人和职业机会的大门。

    我非常感谢您花时间阅读我的内容，我希望它可以帮助您成长并实现您所有的人生梦想。 让我们联系起来，让未来更加光明，您可以在Limey上找到我的所有主要个人资料。



原文:

Subscribe to my newsletter to stay up to date on all of my upcoming articles. In the future, I plan to do some giveaways and share some more cool exclusives 😉

A long time ago in a galaxy far, far away...

From a young age, I was always fascinated by computers. They seemed to have unlimited potential and you could do so many things with them. For a long time I dreamed of having a career in computers but I did not know what that would be or how that would even look because I was still young and trying to find my way in life. That path started to become a lot clearer when I first experienced what it was like to play games on a computer.

It opened my mind to a whole new world of possibilities that I could never have dreamed of! I even built my own custom computer which was a real learning experience for me. That was the first time I got my hands dirty putting together hardware and then installing all of the drivers and software. I had so much fun playing PC games and still have lots of fond memories from childhood.

Next, I was introduced to game consoles which opened my eyes even wider. The Sega Megadrive was the first console I ever played with and that was followed by The Super Nintendo, Nintendo 64, Playstation, Gamecube, and Xbox. I also owned some Nintendo Gameboys and a Sony PSP. I played a lot of games and became completely addicted and obsessed. It definitely turned me into a geek and got me even more interested in programming. These days I do my gaming on a PlayStation 5 and a laptop.

Gaming is by far the biggest contributor to my passion for programming and this was when I decided that I wanted to be a Game Developer. However, there was a big problem I had no idea how to do that or even which programming languages I should learn...

An introduction to programming

High School

My first introduction to programming happened back in High School but it was very basic and I can barely remember it. We had this class called Information Communication Technology (ICT) and if I recall we learned some basic HTML and CSS. This is when I began to learn about web development and I saw it as an easy pathway to getting into the industry.

College

When I left High School I decided to go to college and study Information Communication Technology (ICT). So essentially I was progressing from what I had initially learned in high school in that one class. In college one of the first programming languages that I learned was Visual Basic. Back then I had a very low level of technical ability and I found the language very challenging to learn. In other classes, we were also taught HTML, CSS and JavaScript.

I found HTML and CSS easy to learn because I had prior experience. The code editor we used was Notepad++ which did not have any of the cool intellisense that modern code editors and IDEs have. So there was no code completion, auto-correct or any extensions available. We had to do it all manually. I remember in one of the classes the teacher wanted us to build a simple webpage with some JavaScript animation. I was completely new to JavaScript and was still learning the basics.

While browsing the internet I recalled some websites that had some really cool animated banners. We were still learning the basics of web development but I had already figured out how to use the inspect element in web browsers to view and edit code. I was able to figure out how to copy the banner code and then get it working in my simple webpage. When my teacher saw it he was amazed because he had no idea how I managed to do something like that 😅

University

University is where my skills really started to level up. I was doing a degree in computing and I had my first introduction to using Java and ActionScript. We were taught how to use a Java-integrated development environment called BlueJ. And I began to learn Adobe Flash and ActionScript. Back in the day Flash websites were still popular until Apple replaced them with a lego block. For those of you who don't know it was no longer possible for Flash websites to run in Safari and on iOS devices and the industry transitioned towards HTML5 and Canvas.

In some of my University modules, I also learned how to do 3D modelling and animation which inspired me even more to be a game developer or to go down that path. I also had some web development experience but I was still unsure which path to go down. Even though I aspired to be a game developer I was still not sure if I should do it 100% or be more of a jack of all trades and do different paths. I did create some Flash games during that period so I had some experience.

I graduated from University in 2008 but unfortunately, it was also the start of The Great Recession. Unemployment was high and I was sort of lost because I did not know what I should be doing.

These are the Adobe Flash games I created.

My first internship

After University I chose to do an internship programme at the same university where I graduated. I was fortunate enough to get an internship role at a company in London. My web development skills were still quite basic but I could build websites. This was my first role, and I was working with HTML, CSS and JavaScript and building web pages as well as updating content on the main website. The lead developer who I worked alongside wanted me to learn PHP and gave me a book to learn from.

I found it quite hard to learn the language because the book was quite technical and at the time there were not a lot of good learning resources online for PHP. And to be honest I was not totally sold on the language either because I was much better at front-end development and had little passion for doing any back-end work. In the end, I did not stay at the company after the internship because it was transitioning into a back-end role which was not my thing back then.

My career so far

With the internship over my focus once again switched to finding work. I did a combination of freelance, contract and permanent roles over the years going back and forth. I gained a lot of experience doing so and my programming skills started to improve a lot. It was not all smooth sailing though I did have some periods when I was between jobs or unemployed which can happen a lot during your career.

Towards the end of 2019, I was working in a non-tech role as a supermarket Delivery Driver but it was only part-time work which did not last... My plan was to get back up to speed with programming because I had been out of the game for about 4 months and my programming skills had become so rusty... But then 2020 happened with the Covid pandemic, and yet another recession...

It actually turned out to be a blessing in disguise because we were all forced to stay at home in lockdown for months. I used that time to get back up to speed with my programming and I up-skilled even more by becoming a Full-Stack Developer. I finally found my passion for back-end development when I improved my Knowledge of Node.js. 2020 was also the year that I became a technical writer and I started to post content on Hashnode, DEV and Medium. I had no plans to build an audience or to become popular at the time I just wanted to be active in the community so that it would increase my chances of finding work and getting noticed by companies.

It took me a year to find stable full-time work in tech again. In 2021 I started to work for my current company CGI as a Software Developer and ironically my social media content was a big part of the reason why I was able to get the role!

Interviews can feel like you are playing a Battle Royale

I have had some really good and really awful interviews over the years. Honestly, the whole interview process is broken in my opinion because it is so unbalanced... Anyway, interviews can be ultra-competitive and usually only one person will get the job so you have to try your best to make yourself the best candidate so that you are the last one standing at the end. Interviews have taught me that you should come prepared no matter what. In some interviews, I was asked if I had anything to show and all I could say was no because my portfolio was dry with not enough content or I chose not to bring my laptop so I could not show them my work.

This is a Fortnite Database Battle Royale application which I created. You can find it online here.

On other occasions, I was fully prepared to answer technical questions but I spent so much time working on them that I forgot to prepare myself for the most simple general questions. Like "what do you know about the company?" and "why did you choose to apply for this role?". They are simple and straightforward but when you are stressed and anxious your mind can go blank trying to answer a question that you never prepared for.

The biggest takeaway that I have had from failed interviews is filling in any missing skills and gaps in knowledge. Always ask for feedback it is incredibly valuable and you can use it to be better prepared for your next interviews. Focus on your weaknesses and make them your strengths. So if you are bad at data structures and algorithms then do lots of practice. If you don't know JavaScript fundamentals then put in the time to get better. Turn your failures into positives and defeats into wins.

The modern way to find work

Finding work today as a programmer can be extremely challenging.

The traditional way to find work could look like 🥱

  • 30+ interviews
  • 6 resume iterations
  • 200+ job applications
  • Networking on LinkedIn

An alternative way to find work could look like 💫

  • Building in public
  • Creating SaaS and PaaS
  • Writing technical articles
  • Networking on LinkedIn and Twitter

If you are new to the industry and transferring to tech looking for your first role I would recommend giving the alternative path a go alongside the traditional route. It is unorthodox and you are thinking outside of the box and doing something that is going to make you stand out from the crowd. Another hot tip that I would give is to go to interviews and then give a presentation. Think about it you are the brand and you are trying to sell yourself and your product.

So if you could build a couple of great applications that are not your standard to-do list app that everybody does. You have a good chance of impressing your interviewer. And you are likely to get bonus points if it's a SaaS or PaaS which is generating an income and a lot of people are using. All of this is highly likely to improve your portfolio and highlight your skill set.

Where I am today

Today I am still working at CGI as a Software Developer and I am passionate about front-end, back-end and mobile development. Technical writing and content creation are also a big part of my life now as I am trying to grow my brand and audience across social media. I have a few ongoing freelance jobs and passive income streams with more ideas for the future.

I'm still not a game developer but I have taught myself some C# and I have known about the game engine Unity for quite some time where C# is one of the main programming languages. So even if it does not become the main career it could still be a really fun side hustle for the future. Because it's already pretty intense trying to find the time to do everything that I'm doing now 😅

Thanks for your time!

Words hold so much power and can literally change your perception of reality. Writing is an incredible medium for sharing thoughts, knowledge and learning which is why I enjoy doing it so much. Connecting and networking is a fantastic way to grow your social network and this can lead to so many doors opening with both personal and career opportunities that can come your way.

I really appreciate you taking the time to read my content and I hope that it helps you to grow and accomplish all of your life dreams. Let's connect and make the future brighter you can find all of my main profiles on Limey.

item.title: 每个开发人员都应该收藏的 18 个有用的 GitHub 存储库 👍💯 | 18 Useful GitHub Repositories Every Developer Should Bookmark 👍💯
item.description:
我经常被问到我会向 Web 开发人员推荐哪些资源和技巧。 我决定整理一份我遇到的一些最有用的 GitHub 存储库的列表。

策划的主要标准是实用性，因此这些存储库包括从学习资源和路线图到最佳实践、系统设计和工具的所有内容。

希望这将帮助尽可能多的开发人员，并且每个人都会发现一些有用的东西来提高他们的技能和工作流程，以便在未来创建很棒的项目。

大量免费提供的编程书籍。

成为软件工程师的完整计算机科学学习计划。

关于各种有趣主题的精彩列表。

成为开发人员的最新路线图。

用 JavaScript 实现的算法和数据结构，带有解释和链接。

为软件工程师精心策划的编码面试准备材料。

基于项目的教程的精选列表。

满足您所有开发需求的简短 JavaScript 代码片段。

具有免费套餐的 SaaS、PaaS 和 IaaS 产品列表。

一长串高级 JavaScript 问题及其解释。

从库存照片、Web 模板到框架、库和工具的资源列表。

可用于提高您的编码技能的应用程序创意集合。

通过从头开始重新创建您最喜欢的技术来掌握编程。

了解如何使用不同的前端和后端构建完全相同的 Medium 克隆。

用于软件和 Web 开发的免费 API 的集合列表。

了解如何设计大型系统。 准备系统设计面试。

掌握命令行，一页一页。

写作一直是我的热情所在，它让我乐于帮助和激励人们。 如果您有任何问题，请随时与我们联系！

在Twitter 、 LinkedIn和GitHub 上联系我！

访问我的博客以获取更多类似的文章。



原文:

I often get asked what resources and tips I would recommend for web developers. I decided to curate a list of some of the most useful GitHub repositories I have come across.

The main criteria for curating was practical usability, so these repositories include everything from learning resources and roadmaps to best practices, system designs, and tools.

Hopefully, this will help as many developers as possible and everyone will find something useful to improve their skills and workflow to create awesome projects in the future.

1. free-programming-books

⭐ GitHub stars 246k+

A huge list of freely available programming books.

2. coding-interview-university

⭐ GitHub stars 230k+

A complete computer science study plan to become a software engineer.

3. awesome

⭐ GitHub stars 216k+

Awesome lists about all kinds of interesting topics.

4. developer-roadmap

⭐ GitHub stars 208k+

Up-to-date roadmap to becoming a developer.

5. javascript-algorithms

⭐ GitHub stars 149k+

Algorithms and data structures implemented in JavaScript with explanations and links.

6. node-best-practices

⭐ GitHub stars 80k+

The Node.js best practices list.

7. tech-interview-handbook

⭐ GitHub stars 77k+

Curated coding interview preparation materials for software engineers.

8. project-based-learning

⭐ GitHub stars 75k+

A curated list of project-based tutorials.

9. 30-seconds-of-code

⭐ GitHub stars 102k+

Short JavaScript code snippets for all your development needs.

10. free-for-dev

⭐ GitHub stars 57k+

A list of SaaS, PaaS, and IaaS offerings that have free tiers.

11. javascript-questions

⭐ GitHub stars 46k+

A long list of advanced JavaScript questions, and their explanations.

12. design-resources-for-developers

⭐ GitHub stars 43k+

A list of resources from stock photos, web templates, to frameworks, libraries, and tools.

13. app-ideas

⭐ GitHub stars 56k+

A Collection of application ideas that can be used to improve your coding skills.

14. build-your-own-x

⭐ GitHub stars 162k+

Master programming by recreating your favorite technologies from scratch.

15. realworld

⭐ GitHub stars 68k+

See how the exact same Medium clone is built using different frontends and backends.

16. public-apis

⭐ GitHub stars 206k+

A collective list of free APIs for use in software and web development.

17. system-design-primer

⭐ GitHub stars 194k+

Learn how to design large-scale systems. Prep for the system design interview.

18. the-art-of-command-line

⭐ GitHub stars 109k+

Master the command line, all in one page.

Writing has always been my passion and it gives me pleasure to help and inspire people. If you have any questions, feel free to reach out!

Connect me on Twitter, LinkedIn and GitHub!

Visit my Blog for more articles like this.

item.title: OAS 是 API 开发人员最关键的工具 | OAS is the most crtitical tool for API Developers
item.description:
如今，API 安全是一个热门话题，越来越多的企业通过 API 向世界公开他们的数据和服务。 因此，对您的 API 进行清晰且易于理解的定义变得越来越重要，这样客户和开发人员才能知道如何使用它。

如果我们已经对 Web API 中的资源有一个很好的定义，我们的客户会很高兴使用它。 但是我们如何告知他们我们的端点、模型、消息错误等呢？

一种方法是使用 OpenAPI 规范，它是定义 REST API 的标准。 有数百篇关于 RESTful API 的精彩文章，因此我不会在本文中深入探讨该主题。
相反，本文将探讨使用 OpenAPI（以前称为 Swagger）的好处，包括根据您的定义生成源代码并将其分发给您的客户的能力。

为什么这甚至有用？

如果您不维护实时 API，则查看此处的值可能会有点困难。 但除了人类和机器可读的价值之外，有助于加快 API 的上市时间，与语言无关等等......

使用方便

当有人第一次探索 OAS 时，他可能会在他们可爱的在线编辑器上徘徊 -

但是，如果您手头没有 OAS 规范，这可能看起来已经很乏味了。 不用担心，因为几乎所有主流语言都已经有了代码到 OAS 生成器，这里有一些我最喜欢的例子：

在 Python 中构建 API 的常用框架是 Django Rest Framework 和 Flask-RESTPLUS。 这些第三方模块称为swagger-express和hapi-swagger 。

如果您喜欢离线工作，VS Code 有一个很好的扩展来编辑您的 API 定义，称为 OpenAPI (Swagger) Editor ( https://github.com/42Crunch/vscode-openapi )。

通过使用此编辑器，您将拥有一个用于编辑 OAS 文件的 GUI，并且您将对其进行实时编辑。

而且，使用 VS 命令，您将拥有一个命令列表，可以帮助您向 OAS 添加新元素：

在我看来，OAS 最重要的特点是它可以提供的安全性。 通过制定人类可读和机器可读的标准 API 规范，您可以（以一种好的方式）打开 API 安全自动化的闸门。 这正是新版本 BLST 开源工具的用武之地。BLST 的工具无缝集成到 CI 管道中，有助于确保关键的 API 缺陷不会进入生产环境。

感谢您花时间阅读我的帖子！
如果您正在寻找有关我们在 BLST 所做工作的任何信息，请查看以下链接：

为我们的Github存储库加注星标并加入我们的Discord 频道中的讨论，以帮助我们使 BLST 变得更好！
现在在BLST免费测试您的 API！



原文:

API security is a hot topic these days, with more and more businesses exposing their data and services to the world via APIs. As a result, it's becoming increasingly important to have a clear and easy-to-understand definition of your API, so that clients and developers can know how to use it.

If we already have a great definition for the resources in our Web API, our clients will be excited to use it. But how do we inform them about our endpoints, models, message errors, etc.?

One way to do this is to use the OpenAPI Specification, which is a standard for defining REST APIs. There are hundreds of fantastic articles on RESTful APIs, so I will not be delving deep into that topic in this post.
Instead, this article will explore the benefits of using OpenAPI (formerly Swagger), including the ability to generate source code from your definition and distribute it among your clients.

Why is this even useful?

If you are not maintaining a live API seeing the value here might be a little more difficult. But beyond the value of it being both Human- And Machine-Readable, Helps speed up API's time to market, Language-Agnostic and more...

Ease of Use

When someone first explores OAS, he will probably wander upon their lovely online editors -

https://editor-next.swagger.io/,
https://editor.swagger.io/.

But if you don't have an OAS spec in hand this may already seem tedious. Don't worry because almost every mainstream language already has a code-to-OAS generator, here are some of my favorite examples:

Python

Common frameworks for building APIs in Python are the Django Rest Framework and Flask-RESTPLUS. Django developers can add the django-rest-swagger module via pip, whereas Flask-RESTPLUS has Swagger support built in. Both are very powerful in terms of automation, so they do not require a lot of configuration.

Javascript/Node

Applications built in Node mostly use Express or HAPI as their framework. Third-party modules called swagger-express and hapi-swagger are available for those.

If you prefer working offline, there is a nice extension for VS Code to edit your API definition called OpenAPI (Swagger) Editor (https://github.com/42Crunch/vscode-openapi).

By using this editor, you will have a GUI to edit your OAS file and you will have it edited in real-time.

And, using VS commands, you will have a list of commands that can help you add new elements to your OAS:

OAS and DevOps

In my opinion, the most important feature of OAS is the security it can provide. By making a standard API specification that is both Human - And Machine-Readable, you open the floodgates (in a good way) to security automation of APIs. And that is precisley where the new version of BLST's open source tool comes in. BLST's tools integrate seamlessly into CI pipelines, helping to ensure that critical API flaws do not make it to production environments.

Thanks for taking the time to read my post!
If you're looking for any info about what we do at BLST check these links out:

Star our Github repo and join the discussion in our Discord channel to help us make BLST even better!
Test your API for free now at BLST!
item.author: chainguns

item.title: 数据库与数据仓库 | DataBase Vs Data Warehouse
item.description:
穆罕默德·拉米兹

发表于数据库和数据仓库根本不一样。 两种管理系统的主要区别在于存储数据的类型。 对数据仓库的需求最早出现在 1970 年。有两种类型的数据。

  • 运营数据
  • 战略信息

简而言之，数据分析存储在数据仓库中，日常使用数据存储在数据库中。 让我们深入了解一下。

什么是数据库？
数据库是一种数据存储管理系统，我们可以在其中存储 TB 级的数据，甚至可以存储 Penta 和 zettabyte 的数据，之后我们可以随时使用这些数据执行不同的操作。 该数据库也有多种类型：

  • 结构数据库
  • 非结构化数据库
  • 分层数据库
  • 图数据库

什么是数据仓库？
数据仓库用于存储业务的见解。 数据仓库保存有关业务的策略和信息。 这里存储实时数据。

了解特定产品的客户年龄组和性别

  • 了解产品需求
  • 了解产品的销售指标

    • 数据仓库的快速定义
    数据仓库是：

    • 时变
    • 一体化
    • 非多功能
    • 面向主题

    细节：
    您可以存储数千年的数据洞察力，集成意味着数据采用结构化格式，如果存储在仓库中，您的数据无法更改，但我们可以永久添加或删除数据。 面向主题意味着可以单独管理特定需求使用的数据。

    数据仓库是数据工程或数据科学领域的重要课题。

    与我联系：
    领英
    一旦暂停，zapeeosheikh 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

    一旦解除暂停，zapeeosheikh 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    一旦未发表，zapeeosheikh 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

    如果 zapeeosheikh 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

    感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 zapeeosheikh：

    举报其他不当行为

    取消标记 zapeeosheikh 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



    原文:

    Muhammad Rameez

    Posted on Aug 23

    Database and Data Warehouse are not the same at all. The major difference in both management systems is the type of storing data. The need for Data warehouses first occurred in 1970. There are two types of data.

    • Operational Data
    • Strategic Information

    In a Nutshell, the Analysis of data is stored in Data Warehouse and the day-to-day use data is stored in the Database. Let's take a deep dive.

    What's Database ?
    A database is a storage management system of data where we can store terabytes of data or even Penta and zettabyte of data that we can use afterward to perform different actions at any time. The database is also of many types :

    • Structural Database
    • Unstructured Database
    • Hierarchical Database
    • Graph Database

    What's Data Wearhouse ?
    Data Warehouse is used to store the insights of Business. Data Warehouse holds Strategies and information about the business. Here stores the real-time data.

    _What is the need for Data Warehouse: _

    • To get to know about the age group and gender of customers for the specific product
    • To know about the demand for the product
    • To know about the sales index of the product

    QUICK DEFINITION OF DATA WAREHOUSE
    Data Warehouse is :

    • Time Variant
    • Integration
    • Non-Versatile
    • Subject Oriented

    Details:
    You can store data insight of thousands of years, Integration means data is in Structural Format and your data cannot be changed if it is stored in the warehouse however we can add or delete the data permanently. Subject Oriented means data for specific requirement usage can be managed separately.

    Data Warehouse is the important Topic in Data Engineering or Data Science Domain.

    Connect with me :
    LinkedIn
    Github

item.title: 脱轨 | Off the rails
item.description:
凯西·埃希特纳赫特

发表于我加入了一个学徒计划，该计划提供免费的训练营，并将参与者锁定在为期 2 年的低薪合同（违反协议罚款 25k）。

尽管他们的条款很糟糕，但这似乎是获得行业经验的可靠方式。 我愿意将自己锁定在这种情况下，因为我想从辅助轮开始我的技术生涯。

让别人为我设定基准、课程和期望是安全的。 这样，如果我最终专注于错误的事情，我可以责怪别人。

我昨天发现训练营推迟了 1 到 10 个月。 我的账单没有休假； 所以是时候找份工作了。

在我有空的时候; 我将努力实现使掠夺性学徒变得不必要的目标。

一旦暂停，caseyeee 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，caseyeee 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，caseyeee 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 caseyeee 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 caseyeee：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 caseyeee 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Casey Echternacht

Posted on Aug 23

I joined an apprenticeship program that offers a free bootcamp and locks participants in a 2-year low-pay contract (25k fine for breaking agreement).

As bad as their terms are, it seemed like a sure-fire way to get industry experience. I was willing to lock myself into this situation because I wanted to start out my tech career with training wheels.

There is safety in having someone else set my benchmarks, curriculum and expectations. That way, if I end up focusing on the wrong things I can blame someone else.

I found out yesterday that the bootcamp is delayed anywhere from 1 to 10 months. My bills aren't taking any time off; so it's time to get a job.

In my free time; I'll be working towards my goal of making that predatory apprenticeship unnecessary.

Once suspended, caseyeee will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, caseyeee will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by caseyeee will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If caseyeee is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

item.title: 什么是 AWS CDK？ (A beginners guide) （初学者指南） | What is the AWS CDK? (A beginners guide)
item.description:
几年来，使用代码在云中配置基础设施一直是最佳实践标准。 与 AWS 控制台中的手动方法相比，企业和组织现在看到了预置 AWS 资源的好处。

从那时起，有两种流行的工具可供您使用 AWS CloudFormation 和 Terraform 等声明性方法定义云资源。

但是，声明式方法的配置可能相当广泛，并导致需要维护以 JSON 或 YAML 编写的非常大的模板，以使您的基础设施作为代码保持最新。

这就是 AWS CDK 发挥作用的地方，它可以改进开发人员的工作流程并帮助他们使用命令式方法预置云基础设施。

在本指南中，您将了解 AWS CDK 是什么，如何使用它来创建自己的基础设施即代码，并在开发过程中应用最佳实践。

目录

什么是 AWS 云开发工具包 (CDK)？

他们使这个项目开源，以便社区能够为代码做出贡献并直接将反馈传递给官方维护者。

打字稿

  • 爪哇
  • 当 AWS CDK 成为 GA 时，这意味着从那时起不会有任何重大更改，并且可以安全地用于生产工作负载。

    为了简化 AWS CDK 的概念，它由 3 个主要构建块组成，允许您自定义基础设施的配置：

    • 应用程序 – 这是您的构造树的根，将所有堆栈和构造整合到一个应用程序中，然后可用于在 AWS 云上进行部署。
    • 堆栈– 类似于 CloudFormation 堆栈。 它是一个单一的单元（模板），以构造的形式保存 AWS 资源，并可用于部署。
    • 构造– 是可以包含单个 AWS 资源或组合的多个 AWS 资源的基本构建块。 您可以在自己的构造中自由构建和组合 AWS 资源。

    综合 AWS CDK 应用程序时，结果是一个云程序集，其中包含为您的堆栈（包括应用程序的资产）生成的 AWS CloudFormation 模板。 然后，此程序包可由 AWS CDK CLI 部署到您首选的 AWS 账户和区域。

    正如您在上图中可能已经注意到的那样，您将看到堆栈中显示了多个构造，每个构造都包含不同的 AWS 资源。 这些构造对于 AWS CDK 为开发人员提供的最大好处至关重要。

    在 AWS CDK 中有3 种构建构造的方法，每种方法都有自己独特的功能：

    • 这些属性完全按照其 AWS CloudFormation 对应项进行映射。 如果您可以使用 L2 和 L3 构造构建 AWS 资源，那么使用 L1 构造并没有真正的好处。 这条规则有一个例外……如果 L2 或 L3 构造不允许您通过其 API 自定义某个属性，那么您可以使用逃生舱口来修复它。
    • 例如，您可以将多个资源组合在一起，例如链接到 RDS 和 Application Load Balancer (ALB) 的 EC2 实例。 您可以使用几行代码来初始化这组资源，这允许您在多个项目或堆栈中重用这些模式。

    因为它允许您使用自己的开发工具更快地开发 AWS 资源，我使用 CDK 进行开发和迭代的速度比单独使用 AWS CloudFormation 快得多。 使用我公司的本地软件工具共享和分发 AWS CloudFormation 模式的能力提高了所有 Intuit 账户的一致性和标准化。 更重要的是，AWS 已经让这个项目开源了。 这最终使客户能够直接为 AWS CloudFormation 的未来做出贡献并加以改进。

    以下是可改善您的开发周期的最佳AWS 云开发工具包功能：

    • 支持多种编程语言
    • 使用您自己的开发工具来提高您的 AWS CDK 项目的代码质量。
    • 在为您的项目创建新的 AWS 资源时，您不必重新发明轮子，因为您可以利用构建中心来查找和共享 L2 和 L3 CDK 构建
    • 将您的基础架构、应用程序、配置和部署存储在一个 CDK 应用程序下的单个存储库中。
    • 由于 AWS CDK 是开源的，因此能够为开发人员团队做出贡献并向其传递反馈。
    • 能够使用几行 Typescript 代码生成包含 1000 行的 CloudFormation 模板
    • 能够创建递归函数，允许您迭代地生成多个 AWS 资源，例如生成 5 个 AWS Lambda 函数，而无需编写相同的代码 5 次。

    当 AWS CDK 出现时，我看到发生了巨大的转变，并且形成了一个社区，在这里可以共享结构，人们讨论新想法并分享他们的经验。

    早在我使用 CloudFormation 的日子里，我还没有见过这样一个社区专注于共享 CloudFormation 模板，而不是在 GitHub 上共享代码。

    根据我多年来广泛使用这两种服务的DevOps 工程师的经验，我会说我更喜欢 AWS CDK 而不是 AWS CloudFormation，原因如下：

    • 通过使用包含合理默认值和最佳实践安全策略的 L2/L3 构造来加快新资源的开发时间
    • 能够使用选择的编程语言来定义我的基础设施，而不必使用 YAML 或 JSON 严格声明资源
    • 花时间掌握一门编程语言比花时间为 CloudFormation 编写 YAML 对你的职业生涯更有用。 您日复一日地学习编写代码的技能更容易转移到其他技术工作职能中。

    最后，AWS CDK 只是 AWS CloudFormation 的转译器，它使用cdk synth将您的 TypeScript 源定义转换为将使用 AWS CloudFormation 部署的 JSON 模板。

    因此，最佳实践是使用cfn-lint和checkov等工具验证输出，以防止将基础架构错误配置为代码。

    拥有 AWS CDK 之类的抽象可能很棒，但前提是您了解底层概念。 如果您不知道 CloudFormation 是如何工作的，那么它可能对您的开发周期不利，因为您会花费更多时间进行调试并且不了解为什么事情可能无法正常工作。

    如何开始使用 AWS CDK？

    要开始使用 AWS CDK，您首先需要在您的机器上安装 AWS CDK 工具包，您可以使用终端中的节点包管理器来全局安装包：

     ➜ npm install -g aws-cdk
     新增180个包，7s审核181个包
发现0个漏洞
~ took 7s ~ 花了 7s


    • 原文:

    Provisioning infrastructure in the Cloud using code has been a best practice standard now for a couple of years now. Businesses and organizations now see the benefit of provisioning AWS resources compared to the manual approach in the AWS console.

    From that point on there were two popular tools available that allowed you to define Cloud resources using a declarative approach such as AWS CloudFormation and Terraform.

    However, the configuration of a declarative approach can be rather extensive and results in very large templates written in JSON or YAML that need to be maintained in order to keep your infrastructure as code up-to-date.

    That’s where AWS CDK comes in to improve the workflow of developers and help them provision Cloud infrastructure using an imperative approach.

    In this guide, you’ll learn what AWS CDK is, how you can use it to create your own infrastructure as code, and apply the best practices during development.

    Table of Contents

    What is the AWS Cloud Development Kit (CDK)?

    AWS CDK stands for Cloud Development Kit and is a framework developed by AWS that allows you to define and provision Cloud infrastructure in code and deploy it through AWS CloudFormation.

    AWS publicly released AWS CDK (v.0.8.0) in beta back in 2018 as an alternative approach to developing AWS resources programmatically using TypeScript.

    First public beta release of AWS CDK

    They made this project open-source so that it enabled the community to contribute to the code and directly pass on feedback to the official maintainers.

    In December 2021 during Re:Invent Werner Vogels announced that AWS CDKv2 became generally available (GA) and currently supports the following programming languages:

    • TypeScript
    • JavaScript
    • Python
    • Java
    • C#
    • Go (Developer preview)

    When AWS CDK became GA this meant that there wouldn’t be any breaking changes from that point on and it could safely be used for production workloads.

    AWS CDK Concept

    To simplify the concept of AWS CDK is that it consists of 3 major building blocks that allow you to customize the provisioning of your infrastructure:

    • App – That is the root of your construct tree and consolidates all stacks and constructs in one application that can then be used to deploy on AWS Cloud.
    • Stack – is similar to a CloudFormation stack. It’s a single unit (template) that holds the AWS resources in the form of constructs and can be used for deployment.
    • Construct – is the basic building block that can contain a single AWS resource or multiple AWS resources combined. You’re free to build and combine AWS resources in your own constructs.

    AWS CDK Concept architecture diagram

    When an AWS CDK application is synthesized, the result is a cloud assembly, which contains the generated AWS CloudFormation templates for your stacks including the assets of your application. This package can then be deployed by the AWS CDK CLI to your preferred AWS account and region.

    AWS CDK construct concept

    As you might’ve noticed in the previous diagram, you’ll see multiple constructs displayed within the stacks, each containing different AWS resources. These constructs are fundamental to the biggest benefit that AWS CDK provides to the developer.

    There are 3 ways to build constructs in AWS CDK, each with its own unique features:

    • L1 Constructs are basically the AWS CDK version of AWS CloudFormation resource types. The properties are mapped exactly as its AWS CloudFormation counterpart. There’s no real benefit to using L1 constructs if you can build AWS resources using L2 and L3 constructs. There’s one exception to this rule… if an L2 or L3 construct doesn’t let you customize a certain property via its API, then you can fix that using an escape hatch.
    • L2 Constructs are AWS resources developed by the AWS CDK team that encapsulate L1 constructs using pre-configured security measures in place including sensible defaults. The primary benefit of L2 constructs is that you’re able to create secure AWS resources with a few lines of code.
    • L3 Constructs (also called patterns) are a group of L2 constructs combined to deliver a full solution. For example, you can group multiple resources together like an EC2 instance that is linked to an RDS and an Application Load Balancer (ALB). You can initialize this group of resources using a few lines of code, which allows you to reuse these patterns in multiple projects or stacks.

    What are some of the features of AWS CDK?

    The AWS CDK is a major step for developers to control and automate AWS infrastructure. Because it allowed you to develop AWS resources faster using your own development tools speed at which I can develop and iterate using CDK is significantly faster than using AWS CloudFormation alone. The ability to share and distribute AWS CloudFormation patterns using my company’s native software tools increases the consistency and standardization across all Intuit accounts. Even more importantly, AWS has made this project open source. This finally enables customers to directly contribute to and enhance the future of AWS CloudFormation.

    Here are the best AWS Cloud Development Kit features that improve your development cycle:

    • Supports multiple programming languages
    • Use your own development tools to enhance the code quality of your AWS CDK project.
    • You don’t have to reinvent the wheel as you create new AWS resources for your project because you can make use of the construct hub where you can find and share L2 and L3 CDK constructs
    • Store your Infrastructure, application, config, and deployment in a single repo under one CDK app.
    • Ability to contribute and pass feedback to the developer team since AWS CDK is open source.
    • Ability to generate CloudFormation templates containing 1000s of lines with a few lines of Typescript code
    • Ability to create recursive functions that allow you to iteratively generate multiple AWS resources e.g. generate 5 AWS Lambda functions without writing the same code 5 times.

    AWS CDK vs CloudFormation

    When AWS CDK came out I saw a huge shift happening and a community gets formed where constructs were shared and people discuss new ideas and share their experiences.

    Back in the days when I used CloudFormation, I haven’t seen such a community that focused on sharing CloudFormation templates for instance, other than code shared on GitHub.

    From my own experience as a DevOps engineer having used both services extensively over the years, I would say that I prefer AWS CDK over AWS CloudFormation for the following reasons:

    • Speed up the development time of new resources by using L2/L3 constructs that contain sensible defaults and best practice security policies
    • Ability to use a programming language of choice to define my infrastructure instead of having to strictly declare resources using YAML or JSON
    • Spending time mastering a programming language is more useful for your career than spending time writing YAML for CloudFormation. The skills you learn writing code day in and day out are more transferable to other technical job functions.

    One of the AWS community members made a good point about what many would consider a unique selling point of AWS CDK:

    “1500 lines of CloudFormation became 14 lines of CDK” It’s important to understand that the deployed application still has 1500 lines’ worth of operations and maintenance ownership, not 14

    — Ben Kehoe (@ben11kehoe) December 2, 2021

    In the end, AWS CDK is just a transpiler for AWS CloudFormation by using cdk synth to turn your TypeScript source definition into a JSON template that will be deployed using AWS CloudFormation.

    Therefore it’s a best practice to validate the output by tools such as cfn-lint and checkov to prevent misconfiguration of your infrastructure as code.

    Having an abstraction such as AWS CDK can be great but only if you understand the underlying concepts. If you don’t know how CloudFormation works, it might be detrimental to your development cycle as you spend more time debugging and not understanding why things might not work.

    How do I get started with AWS CDK?

    To get started with AWS CDK you first need to install the AWS CDK toolkit on your machine, you use the node package manager in your terminal to install the package globally:

    ➜ npm install -g aws-cdk 
    added 180 packages, and audited 181 packages in 7s
found 0 vulnerabilities
~ took 7s
item.author: dannysteenman

item.title: rolltoaster - 用于高级任务管理的游戏化 Discord 机器人。 | rollertoaster - A gamified Discord bot for advanced task management.
item.description:
嘿，开发者，

由于许多组织在过去两年中将其工作转移到了 Discord 等社交平台，因此组织活动现在面临着有效分配任务和跟踪正在完成的工作的新挑战。 作为不同组织的一部分，我们注意到的两个常见问题是管理不善和缺乏协调。

为了解决这个问题，我和@mihiraggarwal一起为您带来了rollertoaster，这是一个游戏化的discord 机器人，它可以帮助基于discord 角色有效且高效地分配任务，并跟踪所有已完成的工作。 你可以在github 上查看。

几个主要特点：

  • 基于角色的任务分配
  • 个人用户资料
  • 待办事项状态
  • 任务的个性化验证
  • 基于会员赚取的积分作为奖励的游戏化排行榜

不要忘记在producthunt 上为我们的产品投票。

我们非常感谢您的反馈，并愿意回答任何问题！

一旦暂停，gigabitepro 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，gigabitepro 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，gigabitepro 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 gigabitepro 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 gigabitepro：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 gigabitepro 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Hey DEV,

Since many organizations have shifted their work to social platforms such as Discord in the last two years, organizing events now come with the newfound challenge of effectively assigning tasks and keeping track of the work being done. Being a part of various organizations ourselves, two common issues we noticed were mismanagement and lack of coordination.

To combat this, I, along with @mihiraggarwal bring to you, rollertoaster, a gamified discord bot that helps effectively and efficiently assign tasks based on discord roles and keeps a track of all the work done. You can check it out on github.

Few key features:

  • Roles-based tasks assignment
  • Individual user profiles
  • Todo status
  • Personalised verification of tasks
  • Gamified leaderboard based on points earned by members to act as incentives

Don't forget to upvote our product on producthunt.

We'd really appreciate feedback and would love to answer any questions!
